subject: feat(structure): auto-create/delete sections when moving cells across rows/columns
body:
moveCellToSection now handles three cases atomically via _cellSwap:
- Normal: source has 2+ cells, target exists → move cell, source shrinks
- Delete source: source has 1 cell, target exists → move cell, remove empty section
- Create target: no adjacent section, source has 2+ cells → create new section at edge

Added canMoveCellToSection() and getMoveSectionLabel() helpers for cleaner UI logic.
Buttons now show dynamically based on what operations are possible, with "To New Row/Col"
labels when creating a section vs "To Row N" when moving to existing.

Tags: structure, layout, cells, ux
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: neutral
Epic: layout-structure-controls
Semver: minor
